title: Banisteria portillana
intro: Banisteriopsis caapi (also known as Banisteria portillana), a type of vine native to the Amazon rainforest, is a plant used in traditional medicine and shamanic rituals. The health benefits of Banisteriopsis caapi include its ability to act as a potent MAO inhibitor, allowing the body to absorb the psychoactive compounds of other plants, as well as its potential to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions related to stress. The bioactive constituents of Banisteriopsis caapi include alkaloids such as harmine, harmaline, and tetrahydroharmine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Banisteriopsis caapi include ayahuasca, a traditional Amazonian brew used for spiritual and medicinal purposes, as well as extracts and tinctures used in modern herbalism.
uses: Banisteria portillana, also known as the Panama tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
